title: Social Media Marketing for Practical Creators
subtitle: A Professional Training Manual for Beginners (Sixth Edition – Updated 2026)

modules.title: Module One | Foundation
modules.subtitle: Social Media for Product‑Based Businesses
modules.overview: Social media is your digital storefront. In this module, students learn how visuals replace physical product touch, and why presentation is the fastest way to build trust with first‑time buyers.

lessons.title: Introduction to Social Media (Business Terms)
lessons.body: Social platforms act as marketing channels, customer communication tools, brand representation spaces, and sales support. Every post sends a message about quality and professionalism.

lessons.title: Personal Use vs Business Use
lessons.body: Personal posting is casual and spontaneous. Business posting must be intentional, consistent, and customer‑focused, with clean visuals and a clear purpose.

lessons.title: The Role of Visuals in Selling
lessons.body: Customers judge products within seconds. High‑quality images and video build confidence, while poor visuals reduce perceived value.

lessons.title: Common Beginner Mistakes
lessons.body: Poor lighting, cluttered backgrounds, inconsistent branding, and posting without a plan can all hurt sales even when the product is great.

modules.title: Module Two | Brand Groundwork
modules.subtitle: Preparing Your Business for Social Media
modules.overview: Branding is the overall impression customers have of your business. This module builds a simple, repeatable system for colours, fonts, and presentation.

lessons.title: Beginner Branding
lessons.body: Branding is clarity and repetition: visual style, tone, colour choices, and consistency across platforms.

lessons.title: Business Name & Presentation
lessons.body: Names should be easy to read, pronounce, and scale beyond one product type. Presentation should look professional everywhere your name appears.

lessons.title: Colour & Font Consistency
lessons.body: Choose two main colours and one accent. Use a decorative font for headings and a clean font for details to keep designs readable.

lessons.title: Preparing for Photography
lessons.body: Before the camera, ensure products are clean, designs are well‑spaced, and brand elements are consistent.

modules.title: Module Three | Product Photography
modules.subtitle: Creating Images That Increase Product Value
modules.overview: Photography replaces physical interaction. This module shows how to create professional product photos using a phone and simple lighting.

lessons.title: Why Photography Matters
lessons.body: Images communicate quality, pricing value, and professionalism. Poor photos can make handmade items look unfinished.

lessons.title: Phone Photography Basics
lessons.body: A clean lens, correct lighting, and stable framing are more important than expensive cameras.

lessons.title: Angles & Composition
lessons.body: Use flat lay for labels and packaging, 45‑degree for depth, and close‑ups for craftsmanship details.

lessons.title: Lighting & Backgrounds
lessons.body: Natural window light, neutral backgrounds, and minimal props create clean, trustworthy visuals.

modules.title: Module Four | Product Videography
modules.subtitle: Using Video to Build Trust and Increase Sales
modules.overview: Video shows texture, scale, and use in real time. Short, clear videos build trust faster than images alone.

lessons.title: Why Video Performs Better
lessons.body: Platforms prioritize video. It keeps attention longer and helps customers understand how a product works.

lessons.title: Beginner Equipment
lessons.body: A phone, natural light, and stability are enough. Use a tripod or stable surface to avoid shaky footage.

lessons.title: Effective Video Types
lessons.body: Rotation shots, close‑ups, in‑use demos, and before‑and‑after clips are simple and effective.

lessons.title: Length & Format
lessons.body: Keep most clips between 5–15 seconds with one clear action or feature per video.

modules.title: Module Five | Design for Cutting & Craft Tools
modules.subtitle: Creating Clean, Professional Designs
modules.overview: Design quality impacts perceived value. This module focuses on readability, spacing, and font use for clean cuts.

lessons.title: Fonts & Readability
lessons.body: Use one decorative font and one clean font. Too many fonts make designs look messy and hard to cut.

lessons.title: Commercial‑Use Fonts
lessons.body: Use fonts with clear licenses. Recommended sources include Google Fonts and Font Squirrel.

lessons.title: Spacing & Layout
lessons.body: Increase letter spacing slightly and leave breathing room around text for a polished look.

lessons.title: Common Design Mistakes
lessons.body: Over‑decorating, poor spacing, and unreadable fonts reduce quality even with perfect cutting.

modules.title: Module Six | Platforms for Selling Products
modules.subtitle: Choosing the Right Platforms
modules.overview: Focus on platforms that support visuals and direct communication. Consistency matters more than being everywhere.

lessons.title: Platform Purpose
lessons.body: Use platforms based on how they serve your product: visibility, trust‑building, or direct sales.

lessons.title: Facebook & Instagram
lessons.body: Facebook supports community and communication. Instagram builds visual trust through a clean feed.

lessons.title: WhatsApp for Sales
lessons.body: Use WhatsApp for inquiries, confirmations, and follow‑ups. Keep responses professional and organized.

modules.title: Module Seven | Content Creation & Posting
modules.subtitle: Building Engaging Posts
modules.overview: Great content highlights product value, tells a clear story, and invites interaction.

lessons.title: Text, Image, and Video Posts
lessons.body: Pair text with visuals, keep copy concise, and use video for trust‑building and demonstrations.

lessons.title: Captions & Hashtags
lessons.body: Start with a hook, explain the benefit, and end with a CTA. Use a mix of broad and niche hashtags.

lessons.title: Batching & Scheduling
lessons.body: Create content in weekly batches, schedule posts, and review for consistency before publishing.

modules.title: Module Eight | Selling Digital Templates
modules.subtitle: Creating and Selling Digital Products
modules.overview: Digital templates sell repeatedly without restocking. This module covers formats, packaging, and pricing.

lessons.title: What Sells & File Types
lessons.body: Labels, stickers, and social graphics sell well. Include SVG, PNG, and PDF for compatibility.

lessons.title: Packaging & Licensing
lessons.body: Deliver zipped files with instructions. Clearly state usage rights to avoid confusion.

lessons.title: Pricing Strategy
lessons.body: Start with entry pricing, test demand, and bundle for value. Price based on effort and uniqueness.

modules.title: Module Nine | Metrics & Improvement
modules.subtitle: Using Data to Refine Your Strategy
modules.overview: Metrics show what content works. Beginners should focus on reach, engagement, and follower growth.

lessons.title: Simple Metrics That Matter
lessons.body: Track reach, engagement, and clicks. Avoid vanity metrics that don’t connect to sales.

lessons.title: Using Insights
lessons.body: Review weekly, identify top posts, and replicate what performs best.

lessons.title: Common Misunderstandings
lessons.body: High followers don’t guarantee sales. Focus on quality engagement and consistent improvement.

modules.title: Module Ten | Final Project & Portfolio
modules.subtitle: Applying What You’ve Learned
modules.overview: The final project ties everything together: a branded product, visuals, and a portfolio you can use beyond the course.

lessons.title: Final Deliverables
lessons.body: Create one branded product, a 4‑angle photo set, one short video, and a polished social post.

lessons.title: Quality Standards
lessons.body: Maintain consistent branding, clean visuals, and clear presentation throughout.

lessons.title: Portfolio Beyond the Course
lessons.body: Update regularly and use it to pitch services, build credibility, and attract customers.
